name: slice_split
description: Slice tool 'Split' option (mesh.sliceTool, task 0273 S7). A cube (8v/6f) cut by the plane through the drawn Start->End line perpendicular to the work plane (headless work-plane normal = default world XZ, +Y). The line runs along Z through the origin, so the plane normal is ||X through X=0 (case midX of the S0 slice.json golden). With Split OFF (default) the cut is the S0 connected cut: 12v/10f, one closed shell (the 4 crossing verts at x=0 are SHARED between the +x and -x sub-faces). With Split ON each of the 4 crossing verts is DUPLICATED into a coincident lo/hi pair, so the single connected cut loop becomes TWO distinct boundary edge-loops and the cube splits into two disconnected sections along the cut: 16v/24e/10f (the 4 extra verts sit exactly on top of the 4 crossing verts; the 4 interior loop edges become 8 boundary edges — proof the two sides are disconnected along the cut). Faces stay 10 because splitting duplicates verts, not faces (Cap Sections is explicitly OFF here so the two boundary loops stay OPEN; caps is exercised by the slice_caps golden). Reuses the Loop Slice lo/hi seam-pair split machinery (Mesh.cutByPlaneEx -> splitAlongCutLoop) fed the plane-cut loop instead of an edge-ring loop; mirrors the loop_slice_split golden's assertions. The stronger topological proof (boundary-edge count, component count, coincident seam pairs) lives in the source/mesh.d cutByPlaneEx kernel unittest.
source: Analytic. Cube +/-0.5; plane law = math.planeForSlice(start, end, +Y). Line along Z -> plane normal ||X through origin (X=0). New crossing verts are edge midpoints (t=0.5); Split ON duplicates each into a coincident copy (verts 12-15 coincide with 8-11). Split geometry law is connectivity-correct by construction (Mesh.cutByPlaneEx, task 0273), no reference-app numeric parity needed.
